name: pretrial_bail_risk_score_proxy_income_disparity
premise: A DNA hit from a genealogy database implicates a relative; defense says the investigative path violated departmental policy. In Criminal Justice, the situation tagged internally around pretrial bail risk score proxy income disparity has converged with local pressure in the Oakline corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Engineering says a full fix needs a maintenance window; operations says waiting will predictably increase harm in the interim. Middle management filtered an escalation; the executive on point is hearing some details for the first time on this bridge. A public briefing is scheduled; if no coherent position exists, staff will improvise talking points from conflicting drafts. No option cleanly satisfies both justice and mercy; the least-wrong path requires naming who absorbs downside if the bet is wrong.

agents.name: Casey Grant
agents.identity: I am Casey Grant, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Criminal Justice. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat mercy as disposable collateral every time justice spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Ivan Tran
agents.identity: I am Ivan Tran, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Criminal Justice. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so justice cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes mercy in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will sequence a conservative rollout: smallest blast radius first, documented exceptions only, and real appeals paths—even if justice advocates call that foot-dragging.
I will push a fast, organization-wide lever because partial fixes may leave the worst harm untouched—even if that compresses protections for mercy.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Silence will be interpreted as a decision by everyone outside this room. Casey Grant must choose under justice versus mercy. What does Casey Grant do?
